column1: # Front-door pm-compat conformance matrix — the canonical cell list.
# One row = one assertion the harness runs. See README.md for the full design.
# Columns (TAB-separated):
#   id          unique slug  <incumbent>-<surface>-<case>
#   incumbent   fixture identity: nub npm pnpm10 pnpm11 yarn1 yarnberry bun
#                 or "-" for an incumbent-invariant cell (uses fixtures/nub)
#   surface     config-read | config-write | env-bridge | env-gate | run-flag
#   mode        doc  = assert documented behavior, hermetic, every-PR leg
#               ref  = ALSO diff vs real PM under REF=1 (opt-in leg)
#   assert      assertion verb + args (see README "Assertion verbs")
# FIRST SLICE: the surfaces that reached users (run reporter/regex/env-file,
# config read+write home per incumbent, the npm_config_* bridge). Deferred
# cells are listed as TODO comments so a new finding ADDS a row here instead of
# resurfacing ad-hoc.
# ── run/exec flags (incumbent-INVARIANT — nub's own CLI, not identity-gated) ──
# The exact surface that churned: reporter-silent, regex selection, --env-file,
# multi-script semantics. Each is a documented-behavior assertion; the three
# high-traffic ones (reporter, regex, env-file) also carry a real-PM diff.
run-echo-default
run-echo-silent-flag
run-reporter-silent-flag
run-reporter-env-silent
run-loglevel-env-not-honored
run-multi-script-first-only
run-regex-selection
run-env-file-injects
run-missing-script-errors
# ── config READ honored — seed the incumbent's OWN config, assert nub reads it ─
# registry is the universal scalar every incumbent reads from .npmrc; it is the
# cleanest cross-incumbent read probe (config get registry reflects it).
nub-config-read-npmrc
npm-config-read-npmrc
pnpm10-config-read-npmrc
pnpm11-config-read-npmrc
yarn1-config-read-npmrc
bun-config-read-npmrc
# ── config READ symmetric boundary — a FOREIGN branded file is NOT read ───────
# Under a non-pnpm incumbent nub must ignore a pnpm-NAMED file (the gate is on
# the NAME). npm fixture + a pnpm-workspace.yaml carrying a leak registry → must
# be ignored. POSITIVE CONTROL (3rd operand): also seed the project .npmrc with a
# distinct registry; nub must return THAT (proves the reader is live and picked
# the right file — not vacuously returning the default because nothing was read).
npm-config-ignores-pnpm-yaml
# ── config WRITE home — set a non-shared scalar, assert where it lands ─────────
# pnpm-11 routes non-shared scalars to pnpm-workspace.yaml; everyone else to the
# project .npmrc (neutral home, never a pnpm-branded file). store-dir is a
# non-shared scalar (no auth/registry semantics); the harness greps for the
# distinctive VALUE (the key is normalized kebab→camel in the yaml home, so a
# key-literal grep would miss — the value is the stable witness).
nub-config-write-npmrc
npm-config-write-npmrc
pnpm10-config-write-npmrc
pnpm11-config-write-yaml
bun-config-write-npmrc
# ── env: the npm_config_* bridge (neutral, honored under EVERY incumbent) ─────
# The bridge reaches the install-time RESOLVER, not the config-display path
# (verified: `config get registry` does NOT reflect npm_config_registry — that
# command reads config FILES). So the bridge is observed at install time: point
# npm_config_registry at an unreachable host and assert the resolver tries THAT
# host (the failure names it). ref-mode: it touches the (mocked) network, so it
# runs on the opt-in leg; the doc-side witness is the negative below.
nub-env-bridge-registry
# Documented NEGATIVE (hermetic): npm_config_registry does NOT surface through
# `config get registry` (it's a resolver-path knob, not a config-file value).
# Pinning this stops a future change from silently making config-display honor
# env (which would mask the real bridge path and confuse `config get` semantics).
nub-env-bridge-not-in-config-get
# ── env: branded gating (AUBE_* never read under nub — brand boundary) ────────
# Must target a setting aube DOES read from an AUBE_* env in standalone mode AND
# surfaces via `config get` — else the cell is vacuous. The original AUBE_REGISTRY
# cell proved NOTHING: aube's `registry` has sources.env=[] (settings.toml:633),
# so no env is read at all — it'd pass even against an aube that fully honored
# AUBE_*. `storeDir` is the right target: sources.env=[…,"AUBE_STORE_DIR"],
# sources.npmrc=["store-dir","storeDir"] (settings.toml:1102-1103), so under
# STANDALONE aube the env WOULD win — the nub embedder profile (env_prefix=None)
# must suppress it. Verified vs the binary: `config get store-dir` surfaces a
# value and AUBE_STORE_DIR is ignored under nub.
nub-env-gate-no-aube
# ── DEFERRED / scaffolded (add a row when the gap surfaces or the audit lands) ─
# config-read: pnpm.* fields honored under pnpm only; bunfig honored under bun;
#   yarnrc.yml honored under yarn-berry (doc-mode; host yarn is v1).
# env-gate: pnpm_config_* honored under pnpm, ignored elsewhere;
#   BUN_CONFIG_REGISTRY honored under bun only.
# config-write: per-field incumbent-aware shared-ness (pm-config-field-level-audit).
# lockfile round-trip: covered by tests/conformance/run.sh + aube-conformance.
